title: user — compliance evidence
description: Profile is the PII cluster. Provisioning is where compliance floors bite (age gate at User creation). Erasure is where retention windows matter. This module carries the biggest concentration of GDPR + COPPA + FERPA responsibilities in the identity tier.

controls.evidence: Profile fields are used only for User identification + personalisation (display + locale + timezone). They are never resold, never joined with third-party enrichment, never exposed to Application B when the User is on Application A.

first_name + last_name required only for adult principals; phone optional; DOB stored once on Identity + denormalised to Profile only for query performance (both accessible via the identity_id FK). No collection of address / SSN / gender / nationality on the base module — those are domain-module concerns.
`GET /api/v1/me/profile` returns the caller's own Profile unredacted. Subject requests over-the-wall export via `access::data-export` (Wave 1c).
`PATCH /api/v1/me/profile` lets the subject correct their own record for non-DOB fields. DOB requires admin action + audit trail entry.

`POST /api/v1/users/{user}/erase` soft-deletes; `PurgeErasedUsersJob` finalises after the hold window (default 30d, up to 90d per tenant setting). All child rows CASCADE via FK on hard-delete. See retention.json for the timeline.
Machine-readable JSON export via `access::data-export`. Includes the full Profile blob + every TenantMember row.

Profile PII is behind the redactor by default; the `profile.view.pii` permission is opt-in per admin role. Minor subjects gate even harder via `profile.view.pii.child` requiring an active AthleteGuardian link.
Every Profile mutation is `Auditable`; the audit row carries actor, timestamp, changed_fields, and the request_id.

`age_meets_min` validation rule refuses provisioning when identity->dob puts the subject under 13 AND region.country_code === 'US'. Bypasses require the parent's User to hold `athletes.provision.child` on an `AthleteGuardian` row (Wave 7 sports module wires this).
The provisioning notification includes the tenant's DPA + link to the parent-consent flow when the subject is a minor.
Guardians on an AthleteGuardian row see the full minor Profile via the `profile.view.pii.child` permission and can trigger erasure on behalf of the child.

controls.principle: consent for disclosure of educational records
controls.evidence: Profile fields flagged `educational_record=true` (Wave 7 sports module wires the flag) require an explicit consent grant before being exposed to any caller outside the direct educational relationship. The user module ships the redactor hook; the classification lives per-domain.

ferpa.applicability_note: FERPA applies only when the tenant.business_type = 'school' AND region.country_code = 'US'. The compliance-service resolves this at provisioning time + writes the flag to the tenant's compliance profile.

controls.evidence: Every write path is behind a Policy. Every Policy check is logged in `audits`. Cross-tenant memberships require an explicit UserLinkedToTenant event + admin actor.
PII redactor applies to every egress path unless the caller carries the `.pii` permission. Serverside redaction — clients see the redacted payload before decryption.
The health probes cover FK integrity + orphaned Profile detection + purge queue drain. Failed probes page ops.
Aliased to the GDPR Art. 17 erasure flow.
Aliased to the GDPR Art. 15 access flow.
